meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve can't monetize skills (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't monetize skills. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve Monetization Challenges
content.seo_description: Stop struggling to monetize your projects. Learn how to architect SaaS pricing models using Antigravity and Claude Code in this intermediate builder guide.
content.answer_block: Monetization fails when value is disconnected from pricing. To convert skills into revenue, implement a tiered subscription model that maps features to specific user outcomes. By utilizing Antigravity for rapid prototyping and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you can deploy a scalable, high-conversion pricing engine in hours, not weeks.
content.intro:
You’ve spent months mastering React, Python, and LLM integrations. Your GitHub is a graveyard of functional but invisible projects. The silence isn't a lack of talent; it’s a failure of architecture. You are building features, but you aren't building a business, and that disconnect is why your bank account remains static while your code quality soars.

It is time to bridge the gap between 'coder' and 'founder.' By treating your pricing strategy as a critical technical component—just like your database schema or API layer—you transform your projects from sandbox experiments into revenue-generating assets. We are going to strip away the guesswork and deploy a professional-grade monetization engine.

steps.title: Define Your Value Metric
steps.description: Identify the exact action your users take that provides value. Don't charge for 'time'; charge for 'outcomes' like API calls, tokens processed, or reports generated.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the core user flow of my application and suggest three distinct pricing tiers based on usage-based metrics rather than flat subscriptions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Analyze my current codebase and suggest where to inject usage tracking hooks for Stripe Metered Billing.'

steps.title: Architect the Pricing Schema
steps.description: Create a robust database structure to handle subscriptions. This prevents race conditions and ensures accurate billing cycles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a PostgreSQL schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application that supports both flat-rate and usage-based billing tiers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit 'Create a new migration file to add a subscription_tier column to the users table and a usage_logs table.'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Integration
steps.description: Handle the heavy lifting of payments using Stripe. Focus on secure webhooks to ensure your app stays in sync with payment status.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure Node.js webhook handler for Stripe events, specifically focusing on customer.subscription.updated and invoice.payment_failed.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'npm install stripe && create a webhook handler in src/api/webhooks/stripe.ts'

steps.title: Build the Pricing UI
steps.description: Create a high-converting pricing page that highlights the 'recommended' tier to reduce cognitive load on the user.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a responsive React pricing component using Tailwind CSS that highlights the middle tier and includes a toggle for monthly/yearly pricing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit 'Create a new PricingPage component in components/marketing/PricingPage.tsx with responsive cards.'

steps.title: Automate Subscription Enforcement
steps.description: Use middleware to gate access to premium features based on the user's subscription status stored in your database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Next.js middleware function that checks the user's subscription status and redirects unauthorized users to the upgrade page.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit 'Add a subscription guard middleware in middleware.ts to protect /dashboard routes.'

pro_tips: Always offer a 'Free Tier' that acts as a lead magnet, not a product; keep your best features behind a paywall.
Use 'Price Anchoring' by placing a high-ticket Enterprise tier next to your mid-market plan to make the mid-market price seem like a steal.
Implement 'Graceful Degradation' where users are downgraded to a limited plan rather than having their data deleted upon payment failure.
Never hardcode prices; fetch them dynamically from Stripe's API so you can experiment with pricing changes without redeploying code.
Track 'Churn Reasons' in your database; if a user cancels, trigger a short feedback loop to learn why.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, set up a simple analytics dashboard to track conversion rates. Start A/B testing your pricing copy to see which feature descriptions drive the most signups.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped to stop being a student and start being a founder. Every line of code you write should have a clear path to value. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
